data.title: What's Behind The Drop In Citius Pharmaceuticals?
data.description:
• Citius Pharmaceuticals stock is feeling bearish pressure. Why are CTXR shares down?

Citius Prices Direct Offering At $5 Million

Citius Pharmaceuticals announced a registered direct offering for gross proceeds of $5 million priced at-the-market under Nasdaq rules, with H.C. Wainwright & Co. acting as the exclusive placement agent. The company also noted the release is not an offer to sell or a solicitation to buy securities in any jurisdiction where such activity would be unlawful prior to registration or qualification under applicable securities laws.

The broader market is mixed today, with the Nasdaq up 1.1% and the Dow down 0.3% as market breadth stays slightly negative (advance/decline ratio of 0.8). CTXR's selloff is steeper than the Healthcare sector's -1.3% move, pointing to company-specific pressure on top of a weak sector session.

Citius Stock Remains In Repair Mode

CTXR is sitting near the lower end of its 52-week range ($2.48 high, 63 cents low), which keeps the longer-term chart in "repair mode" after repeated failed rebounds. The stock is trading 11.6% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 16.6% below its 100-day SMA, which suggests near-term sellers still control the trend even after prior bounces.

The moving average convergence divergence (MACD), a trend/momentum measure, is above its signal line with a positive histogram, which leans toward improving momentum versus the prior downswing. In everyday terms, MACD being above the signal line means downside pressure is easing, but it doesn't guarantee a sustained reversal.

The 50-day SMA remains below the 200-day SMA following the death cross in January, a longer-term bearish backdrop that can make rallies more likely to fade. Over the past 12 months, the stock is down 16.35%, which is consistent with a choppy-to-down longer-term tape where financing headlines can hit harder.

  • Key Resistance: $1.00 — a round-number area where rebounds often stall and supply can show up.
  • Key Support: 50 cents — a nearby "line in the sand" if selling pressure continues toward the prior low zone.

Citius Leans Into Oncology Therapies

Citius Pharmaceuticals is a biopharmaceutical company focused on developing and commercializing first-in-class critical care products, with an emphasis on oncology, anti-infectives in adjunct cancer care, and distinct prescription products. The company's strategy targets unmet medical needs while aiming for lower development risk than typical new chemical entities.

Its flagship product candidate is LYMPHIR, an engineered IL-2 diphtheria toxin fusion protein, and the business operates as a single segment centered on targeted oncology therapies. In that context, a $5 million at-the-market registered direct offering matters because it can extend runway for development and commercialization plans, but it can also weigh on the stock near-term as investors reprice dilution risk.

Earnings And Analyst Outlook

Looking further out, the next major catalyst for the stock arrives with the May 13, 2026 (estimated) earnings report.

  • EPS Estimate: Loss of 32 cents (Up from $-1.27 YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: $9.87 million (Up from $0.00 million YoY)
  • Valuation: P/E ratio not meaningful/available (common for development-stage biotech with losses)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy rating with an average price target of $6.00. Recent analyst moves include:

  • D. Boral Capital: Buy (Maintains Target to $6.00) (March 11)

Shares Slip On Friday

CTXR Stock Price Activity: Citius Pharmaceuticals shares were down 21.44% at $0.73 at the time of publication on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Cadence Design Systems Targets Faster Chip Design Cycles To Keep Pace With AI
data.description:
Shares of Cadence Design Systems Inc (NASDAQ:CDNS) rallied in early trading on Friday ahead of the company's first-quarter results, scheduled to be reported on Monday, April 27.

While the company is likely to report total revenues ahead of expectations, its operating margin and earnings could miss consensus estimates, according to Rosenblatt Securities.

The Cadence Design Systems Analyst: Analyst Blair Abernethy reiterated a Buy rating and price target of $360.

The Cadence Design Systems Thesis: The company is likely to report total revenue of $1.452 billion, up 16.9% year-on-year but missing consensus of $1.446 billion, Abernethy said in the note.

Check out other analyst stock ratings.

Cadence Design Systems could generate Hardware-driven Verification growth of around 16% year-on-year, which is commendable given tough comps of around 23% growth in the same quarter in 2025, he added.

The analyst expects the company's non-GAAP operating margin to have remained stable in the first quarter, at 43%, below consensus of 44%. He projected non-GAAP earnings of $1.90 per share, below consensus of $1.92 per share.

"We believe Cadence's broad-based growth drivers in digital chip and analog/PCB electronics design engineering were stable in Q1," Abernethy further wrote.

Abernethy expects Cadence Design Systems' total revenue growth to accelerate to 16% in 2026, from 14% in the previous year. He expects earnings to grow around 11.5% to $7.96 per share.

Cadence Design Systems has provided details of its product strategy, which aims at shortening chip development cycles "to better align with the much faster AI model innovation cycles," the analyst stated.

CDNS Price Action: Cadence Design Systems shares were up 3.93% at $326.67 at the time of publication on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo by wee dezign on Shutterstock

data.title: Westport Fuel Systems Reports Q4 2025 Results: Full Earnings Call Transcript
data.description:
On Friday, Westport Fuel Systems (TSX:WPRT) discussed fourth-quarter financial results during its earnings call. The full transcript is provided below.

This content is powered by Benzinga APIs. For comprehensive financial data and transcripts, visit https://www.benzinga.com/apis/.

The full earnings call is available at https://edge.media-server.com/mmc/p/uxhk9mgz/

Summary

Westport Fuel Systems successfully completed a cybersecurity review, ensuring business continuity and financial reporting integrity.

The company divested its light-duty business, received a $6.5 million payment, and ended the year with $27 million in cash, reflecting improved financial health with low debt.

Total revenue in Q4 2025 was $29.3 million, a 28% increase from the previous year, driven by strong market adoption and OEM trials, despite a yearly revenue drop due to the end of a transitional service agreement.

Adjusted EBITDA for 2025 was negative $17.3 million, and the net loss from continuing operations was $29.6 million, slightly improved from the prior year.

Strategic focus includes expanding the market for its CNG fuel storage and delivery system, with progress in North America and growing opportunities in India and Latin America.

The company plans to showcase its technology at the upcoming Advanced Clean Transportation Expo, with a focus on high-pressure CNG systems and HPDI technology.

Operational highlights include transitioning manufacturing from Italy to Canada and China, leading to temporary margin pressures but expected improvements in 2026.

Management is optimistic about 2026, focusing on disciplined execution, advancing OEM programs, and addressing new market opportunities, especially in North America and China.

Full Transcript

OPERATOR

Ashley Newell

Dan Selai (Chief Executive Officer and Director)

Elizabeth Owen (Chief Financial Officer)

Dan Selai (Chief Executive Officer and Director)

OPERATOR

Manit Dayal (Equity Analyst at HC Wainwright)

Good morning everyone. Thank you for taking my questions. Good morning, Amit. So, Dan, just, you know, on the margin side of things, it looks like, you know, inventory issues and, you know, relocation issues were sort of pressuring margins in the fourth quarter. Do you think we see some bounce back in 1Q and the rest of 2026 on the margin side?

Dan Selai (Chief Executive Officer and Director)

Manit Dayal (Equity Analyst at HC Wainwright)

Understood. For the high pressure control segment, you know, can you talk a little bit about sort of, you know, how maybe the China market or the Indian market, et cetera, you know, the international opportunities you highlighted could start ramping for you? What should we expect in terms of go to market strategy in these geographies?

Dan Selai (Chief Executive Officer and Director)

Manit Dayal (Equity Analyst at HC Wainwright)

Understood this last one for me. Any, any opportunities or possibilities in the power gen or backup power space for you guys?

Dan Selai (Chief Executive Officer and Director)

Well, the interesting you asked. So we've been looking into power gen. We currently supply into power gen. Today we have a customer that used to be Kohler Rolko that we supply out of our high pressure controls business. We see that opportunity growing, you know, with the investments going into power gen across North America and of course globally. We think that there's an opportunity to build out that business and are expected to grow there.

Manit Dayal (Equity Analyst at HC Wainwright)

Understood. That's all I have guys. I'll take my other questions offline. Thank you.

Dan Selai (Chief Executive Officer and Director)

Great, thanks Amit.

OPERATOR

Second, this question or comment comes from the line of Rob Brown from Lake Street Capital. Markets. Mr. Brown, your line is open.

Dan Selai (Chief Executive Officer and Director)

Hi, good morning. Good morning, Rob.

Rob Brown

First question's on the OEM trial at Cispaire. The second oem. I know you can't give a lot of detail, but I think you said this year is sort of when the trial is happening. You know, what's sort of the decision point on that? Is it sort of work this year and then just make decisions and then start potentially ramping into a production model or just sort of the outlines of the process would be helpful.

Dan Selai (Chief Executive Officer and Director)

Rob Brown

Okay, got it. And then back to the high pressure controls business run rate. Get a sense of what's the sort of revenue run rate now that you've gotten the production transition. Is it sort of growing off the Q4 run rate or is it, you know, I guess how much of the Q4 run rate was depressed from that? I guess just a sense of the run rate in that business.

Dan Selai (Chief Executive Officer and Director)

Rob Brown

Okay, thank you. I'll turn it over.

Dan Selai (Chief Executive Officer and Director)

Thank you.

OPERATOR

Thank you again, ladies and gentlemen. If you have a question or comment at this time, please press star one one on your telephone keypad. Our next question or comment comes from the line of Mr. Eric Stein from Craig Hallam Capital Group. Mr. Stein, your line is open.

Dan Selai (Chief Executive Officer and Director)

Hi Dan. Hi, Elizabeth. Good morning. Hey, good morning, Eric.

Eric Stein (Analyst at Craig Hallam Capital Group)

Dan, you touched on HPDI in India and in your prepared remarks, Latin America and some other markets. But in terms of in North America, I mean, I know that's a very high priority. You did mention some trials that you are planning or that the joint venture is planning in Canada. Could you maybe go into that a little bit? Anything you can share and should we assume then that Canada, Canada is kind of the initial spot in North America that you would target?

Dan Selai (Chief Executive Officer and Director)

Eric Stein (Analyst at Craig Hallam Capital Group)

Got it. Okay, I misunderstood that. Thank you for the clarification. So then I guess the follow up then would be just about bringing hpdi, the joint venture, since you just talked about back a cab, but HPDI to North America. Yeah, I would assume that that would be Volvo, right?

Dan Selai (Chief Executive Officer and Director)

Eric Stein (Analyst at Craig Hallam Capital Group)

Yep. No, absolutely. Okay, thanks for that. And just housekeeping for my last question or questions, just. I might have missed it, but did you quantify or estimate what you think the move did in terms of limiting Q4 for the high pressure segment?

Dan Selai (Chief Executive Officer and Director)

Eric Stein (Analyst at Craig Hallam Capital Group)

Okay, thank you.

Dan Selai (Chief Executive Officer and Director)

Perfect. All right. Thanks, Eric. Well, that's all the questions we have for today. I want to thank you for your time, everyone, and have a great, wonderful weekend.

data.title: If You Invested $100 In VanEck Uranium and Nuclear ETF Stock 5 Years Ago, You Would Have This Much Today
data.description:

data.title: Lucid Stock Is On The Move: The Chart Tells An Interesting Story
data.description:
• Lucid Group stock is testing key support levels. Why did LCID hit a new low?

What the Chart Is Signaling

Lucid’s pullback isn't tied to a specific headline. Instead, the stock has been trading below key trend gauges for long enough that even modest selling pressure is enough to push it lower. With shares sitting near their 52‑week low and well under major moving averages, there's very little nearby technical support to slow downside momentum.

The stock is pinned near the bottom of its 52‑week range between $33.70 and $6.21, and the trend remains firmly bearish. Lucid is trading 27.1% below its 20‑day simple moving average and 40.8% below its 100‑day SMA, a setup that typically keeps rallies short‑lived and reinforces a "sell‑the‑bounce" mentality.

Momentum indicators confirm the weakness. The RSI sits at 26.62, which is technically oversold. Oversold conditions can lead to short‑term bounces, but they also reflect persistent selling pressure and a market that hasn't yet found a reason to shift sentiment.

Zooming out, Lucid is down 74.67% over the past 12 months, and both the recent swing low and the 52‑week low were set in April, reinforcing how that month became a key breakdown point in the longer‑term trend.

Volume has been heavier on down days and lighter on up days, a pattern that signals sellers remain in control and buyers are not stepping in with conviction.

Sector Context

Consumer Discretionary is up 0.47% today, making it one of the strongest sectors in the market. Lucid’s decline against that backdrop shows the weakness is stock‑specific rather than sector‑driven. Breadth is also soft, with an advance/decline ratio of 0.6, meaning green indexes aren't offering much protection to names with weak charts.

Key Levels Traders Are Watching

  • Resistance: $8.50 This level sits near the 20‑day SMA zone area where rebounds may run into supply.
  • Support: $6.00 This is the psychological floor just below the current 52‑week low. A breakdown below $6.00 would confirm a fresh leg lower and signal that sellers remain firmly in control.

LCID Shares Are Dropping

LCID Price Action: Lucid shares were down 0.16% at $6.26 at the time of publication on Friday. The stock is trading at a new 52-week low, according to Benzinga Pro.

Image: Shutterstock
